summary: Northstar's regional managers submit weekly updates in inconsistent formats (email, Notion, spreadsheets), requiring 3-4 hours of manual consolidation each Monday. They need an AI-assisted workflow to draft standardized leadership briefings with mandatory human review before posting to Notion.

stakeholders.name: Maya Chen
stakeholders.interests: Reduce time spent consolidating regional updates; ensure accurate risk flagging and action item ownership; maintain team trust in output quality

stakeholders.name: Luis Ortega
stakeholders.interests: Coordinate implementation and ensure customer success with the new workflow

stakeholders.name: Priya Shah
stakeholders.interests: Design and deliver the briefing workflow solution

stakeholders.name: Regional Managers
stakeholders.interests: Submit weekly updates (currently in varied formats)

stakeholders.name: Operations Lead
stakeholders.interests: Review and approve AI-drafted briefings before leadership distribution

risks.description: Team needs to trust the AI output quality before adopting the workflow
Action items may be assigned to incorrect owners if not properly validated
Inconsistent input formats from regional managers may affect extraction accuracy

next_steps.action: Create sample briefing workflow for one region and one weekly briefing
next_steps.notes: Review scheduled with Maya next Friday

open_questions: What is the specific format/template required for the leadership briefing output?
How many regions does Northstar have, and what is the rollout sequence after the pilot?
What approval mechanism will be used in Notion for the operations lead review?
Are there existing data validation rules or owner assignment logic to prevent incorrect action item assignments?
